# Plan: Multi-Component Fixes & Enhancements

## 1. Overview

A cross-cutting stabilization and enhancement pass over Wave Client touching **Collections** (import fidelity, schema versioning, CRUD integrity, move/save UX, smart type detection), **Environments** (Postman import, type selection, versioning), **History** (real-time refresh), **Requests** (editor spacing, copy/paste placement, save shortcut, cancellation, response download), and **General** (versioning strategy, docs). Most items are bug fixes with verified root causes (documented per feature below); the rest are focused enhancements. No formal decomposition strategy is used — this is targeted fix/enhancement work grouped by domain and ordered by dependency (schema versioning lands first because import features stamp versions; UI polish lands last).

**Out of scope (tracked in `.spec-lite/TODO.md`):** resolving `.http` special variables (`@dotenv`, request chaining like `{{login.response.body.$.token}}`), creating *new* folders from the move dialog, automated release tooling (changesets/CI publishing).

## 3. Tech Stack Additions

No additions — see memory. Zod (already standard for boundary validation) covers the new schema work; axios `AbortController` support covers cancellation.

## 4. Data Model (High-Level)

### Domain Concepts

- **Wave Collection Schema (v0.0.1)**: The persisted shape of `Collection` (`packages/core/src/types/collection.ts`). Gains a mandatory, documented `info.version` (already stamped ad hoc as `0.0.1` by `CollectionService`) plus a formal Zod schema validated at import/load boundaries. `info.waveId` is the stable identity; `filename` remains the on-disk key.
- **Wave Environment Schema (v0.0.1)**: The persisted shape of `Environment`. Gains a `version` field and a formal Zod schema, mirroring the collection schema approach.
- **Schema version vs. package version**: schema versions evolve independently from app/package versions (see FEAT-012). A schema version bump implies a documented migration note.

### Conceptual Relationships

- Collection → CollectionItem (tree, arbitrary nesting) → optional AnyCollectionRequest. **Invariant to enforce:** when an item wraps a request, `CollectionItem.name === item.request.name`, and `CollectionItem.id` / `request.id` are stable across rename and move.
- Environment → EnvironmentVariable[] (flat list).

### Storage Strategy

Unchanged: JSON files per collection/environment under the app directory, managed by `packages/shared/src/services/` (CollectionService, etc.). This plan hardens the read/write paths; it does not change the storage medium.

## 5. Interface Design

Feature-by-feature design with verified root causes.

---

### FEAT-001 — Wave Schema Versioning (Collection & Environment)

**Current state**: `CollectionInfo.version` exists but is optional, undocumented, and stamped opportunistically in `CollectionService` (`waveVersion = '0.0.1'`). `Environment` has no version at all. No Zod schemas exist for either.

**Design**:
- Add Zod schemas in `packages/core` (e.g., `src/schemas/collectionSchema.ts`, `src/schemas/environmentSchema.ts`) covering the full persisted shape: `WaveCollectionSchema` and `WaveEnvironmentSchema`, each exporting a `CURRENT_*_SCHEMA_VERSION = '0.0.1'` constant.
- `Environment` type gains `version?: string`; loaders stamp `0.0.1` when absent (same pattern `CollectionService.loadOne` already uses for collections).
- Validate with Zod at the **import boundary** (collection/environment import) and **load boundary** (loadAll/loadOne) per the memory rule "validate all external input with Zod". Validation failures surface as `Result` errors, not throws.
- Documentation: new `docs/schemas.md` (or `docs/schemas/` pair) documenting every field, the version field semantics, and a "version history" section starting at 0.0.1. This is the canonical schema reference going forward.
- All transformers (`Wave`, `Postman`, `HttpFile`, `Swagger`) stamp `info.version` from the constant — remove scattered literal `'0.0.1'` strings (`BaseCollectionTransformer.waveVersion`, `CollectionService.waveVersion` consolidate to the core constant).

---

### FEAT-002 — HTTP File Transformer: full `.http` syntax

**Current state**: `HttpFileTransformer.ts` splits on `###`/`---` and does a single-pass line parse. Gaps vs. the [ASP.NET Core .http spec](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/aspnet/core/test/http-files?view=aspnetcore-10.0#http-file-syntax): no `//` comments, no `# @name` / `// @name` parsing, no file-variable (`@var=value`) line handling, comments between headers/body break parsing, duplicate request names are produced freely.

**Design** (rewrite `parseHttpFile` / `parseRequestBlock`):
- **Separators**: requests delimited by lines starting with `###` (text after `###` is a candidate name). Drop `---` (not part of the spec).
- **Comments**: lines starting with `#` or `//` are comments anywhere outside the body; ignored except for name extraction. Blank lines and arbitrary whitespace are tolerated everywhere outside the body.
- **Request line**: `[METHOD] URL [HTTP-version]` — method optional (default `GET`), HTTP version ignored. Support the standard methods plus `TRACE`/`CONNECT`. Support URL continuation lines (subsequent lines starting with `?` or `&` are appended to the URL, per spec).
- **File variables** (`@name=value` lines): recognized and skipped without error. `{{var}}` references are preserved verbatim in URL/headers/body (no resolution — out of scope).
- **Headers**: `Key: Value` lines after the request line until the first blank line; comment lines interleaved are skipped.
- **Body**: everything after the first blank line following headers, up to the next `###` separator; trailing whitespace trimmed; comments NOT stripped inside the body.
- **Naming strategy** (priority order, then uniqueness pass):
  1. `# @name requestName` / `// @name requestName` (also accept `@name = name` spacing variants) above the request,
  2. otherwise the nearest non-directive comment line above the request (e.g., `### Get Users` separator text or `# Get Users`),
  3. otherwise derive from the URL: strip protocol/variables, keep alphanumerics as space-separated words (e.g., `GET api example com users` → `Get users` style simple name).
  - Uniqueness: maintain a used-name set; on collision append ` 2`, ` 3`, … Both `CollectionItem.name` and `request.name` get the same final name.
- Unit tests in `packages/core/src/test/utils/transformers/` covering: comments everywhere, `@name` variants, variable lines, method-less requests, query continuation lines, multiple requests, duplicate-name dedup, body with `#` characters.

---

### FEAT-003 — Collection Operations Integrity

**Verified root causes**:
1. **Rename doesn't persist (vscode)**: `vsCodeAdapter.saveCollection` fires a `saveCollection` postMessage and optimistically returns `ok(collection)` — but `MessageHandler.handleMessage` has **no `saveCollection` case**. The message is silently dropped. (Web adapter persists correctly via `POST /api/collections`.)
2. **IDs not retained on move/duplicate**: `CollectionService.saveRequest` matches the target by **name** and pushes new items with a fresh `generateUniqueId()`, discarding the moved item's existing `id`. The adapter (`saveRequestToCollection`) sends only `item.request` + `item.name`, dropping item-level fields (`description`, `response`).
3. **Store keyed by name**: `createCollectionsSlice.updateCollection/removeCollection` match on `info.name`, which is exactly the field that changes during rename — fragile and the source of "surprises".

**Design**:
- **MessageHandler**: add `saveCollection` case → `collectionService.save(collection, collection.filename)` (filename required; error if absent). Convert `vsCodeAdapter.saveCollection` to the standard `sendAndWait` request/response pattern so the UI sees real success/failure instead of an optimistic `ok`.
- **ID-based slice operations**: re-key `updateCollection`/`removeCollection` by `filename` (the persistence key, always present after load) with `info.waveId` as the logical identity. Update all callers in `CollectionsPane.tsx` and both `App.tsx` files. Per memory, backwards compatibility is not required — change the signatures cleanly.
- **ID retention**: extend `saveRequestToCollection` (adapter contract, MessageHandler, `CollectionService.saveRequest`, server route) to carry the **whole `CollectionItem`** (id, name, description, request). Match existing items by `id` first (update-in-place), fall back to name match only for legacy callers; never regenerate an id that already exists. Moves therefore preserve `item.id` and `request.id` end-to-end.
- **Name integrity**: a shared helper (e.g., `renameItemInTree` hardened in `collectionParser.ts`) that sets both `CollectionItem.name` and, when present, `item.request.name` atomically. Audit duplicate/save flows for the same invariant.
- **Validations** (on every save/import path, shared util): non-empty trimmed name; sibling-level name uniqueness (case-insensitive) for folders and requests; collection-level name uniqueness across collections; all items have ids. Violations return descriptive `Result` errors shown via notification.
- **Folder-level vs item-level parity**: rename/delete must behave identically for folders and requests (delete folder = subtree delete, already handled by `deleteItem`; verify rename folder updates paths used by expanded-state keys and `sourceRef.itemPath` consumers).
- Regression tests: vscode `MessageHandler.test.ts` (new `saveCollection` case), core slice tests, `CollectionService` tests for id-retention and validation failures.

---

### FEAT-004 — Move & Save Request Dialog Overhaul

**Verified issues** (`RequestSaveWizard.tsx` + `CollectionsPane.tsx`):
- Current path line shows only the folder path (`sourceParentPath`), **not the collection name**, and renders `(Root)` ambiguously — users can't tell where the request currently lives.
- The folder dropdown is labeled "Move to Folder (optional)" and is **hidden entirely** unless `isExistingCollection && folderPathOptions.length > 0`; `folderPathOptions` depends on `searchQuery` exactly matching a collection name, so mid-typing or after "create new" toggling the dropdown vanishes or shows only `(Root)`.
- Folder selection round-trips through `path.join('/')` — folder names containing `/` corrupt the path (split on `'/'` on the way back).

**Design**:
- **Current location**: in move mode, always render `Current location: <Collection> / <Folder> / …` (collection name + full folder path; `<Collection> (root)` when at root) sourced from `sourceCollectionName` + `sourceParentPath`.
- **Destination folder selector**: always visible once a valid destination collection is selected; label `Destination Folder`, default `(Root)`; options are the full folder tree of the **selected** destination collection (any collection, any depth — `getFolderPathOptions` already produces this, the gating logic is the bug). Remove the "(optional)" phrasing; helper text: "Pick a folder or keep (Root)".
- **Robust option values**: key dropdown options by index/JSON path, not `'/'.join`, eliminating the delimiter bug.
- **Save flow parity**: the editor "Save to Collection" flow uses the same component — verify it also exposes the full folder tree (same fix), so saving into existing sub-folders works from both entry points.
- **Move correctness follow-through**: `handleConfirmMoveRequest` keeps its save-then-delete order, but with FEAT-003's id-retention the moved item keeps its identity; add a same-name-exists-at-destination check that errors instead of silently overwriting (current `saveRequest` name-match would overwrite a different request with the same name).
- Component tests updated in `RequestSaveWizard.test.tsx` (current path rendering, folder list for non-source collections, move into sub-folder).

---

### FEAT-005 — Smart Collection Type Detection

**Current state**: `CollectionsImportWizard` calls `detectFormatFromFilename` only (extension + filename keywords). Content-based detection (`detectTransformer`, which runs each transformer's `canHandle`) exists but is unused by the wizard.

**Design**:
- On file select, read the file text immediately (the wizard already reads it at import time) and run **content-based detection first**: try `JSON.parse` → `detectTransformer(parsed)` (Swagger → Postman → Wave priority already encoded in the registry); if not JSON, try YAML heuristic for OpenAPI (`openapi:`/`swagger:` keys) and `httpFileTransformer.canHandle(text)` for `.http` content.
- Fall back to `detectFormatFromFilename` when content detection is inconclusive; final fallback `wave`.
- Keep the manual dropdown as the override (unchanged behavior); helper text updated to "Detected from file content".
- Extract this into a shared `detectFormatFromContent(text: string): ImportFormatType | undefined` in `transformers/index.ts` with unit tests (one fixture per format + ambiguous/garbage cases), so FEAT-006 can mirror it for environments.

---

### FEAT-006 — Environment Import: Postman + Type Dropdown

**Current state**: `EnvImportWizard.tsx` accepts only Wave environment JSON (validates JSON-parseability only). No type selection, no Postman support.

**Design**:
- New lightweight environment transformer module in `packages/core` (e.g., `src/utils/transformers/environmentTransformers.ts`) following the collection transformer pattern but scoped to two formats:
  - `wave`: current JSON shape (single env or array), validated by the FEAT-001 Zod schema.
  - `postman`: schema per the Postman export — map `values[].key/value/enabled` → `EnvironmentVariable` (`type: 'default'`; mark `type: 'secret'` when Postman `type === 'secret'`), `name` → `name`, generate a fresh `id` (do not trust Postman's), stamp `version` per FEAT-001. Detection signals: `_postman_variable_scope === 'environment'` or `values` array-of-`{key,value}` shape.
- `EnvImportWizard` gains an **Environment Type** dropdown identical in UX to the collection wizard: auto-detected from content on file select, manually overridable. Import path converts to Wave format **before** handing to `onImportEnvironments` (so MessageHandler/server import stays Wave-only, mirroring how collection import transforms in the App layer).
- Unit tests: Postman fixture → Wave mapping (enabled flags, secret type), detection, malformed input errors.

---

### FEAT-007 — History Real-Time Refresh

**Verified root cause**: both `App.tsx` files call `storage.saveRequestToHistory(tabRequest)` in `handleSendRequest`, but `setHistory` is only invoked at initial load and manual retry — the History pane shows stale data until reload. Collections/cookies already reload after mutation (e.g., cookies reload after a response sets them).

**Design**:
- Follow the existing pattern: after a successful `saveRequestToHistory`, reload via `storage.loadHistory()` → `setHistory(...)` in both vscode and web `App.tsx`. Extract a small `refreshHistory()` helper (same shape as `handleRetryHistory`) to avoid triplicating the load block.
- Same treatment for `clearHistory`/`deleteHistoryItem` flows if any caller mutates persistence without updating the slice (audit `HistoryPane` callers; `removeHistoryItem` already covers single delete).
- Keep it pull-based (no event bus) — consistent with how the rest of the app refreshes.

---

### FEAT-008 — Response Download Fix

**Verified root cause**: `ResponseBody.handleDownload` calls `onDownloadResponse({ body, fileName, contentType })` — an **object** — but both apps type the callback as `(data: string)`:
- vscode: `new TextEncoder().encode(data)` on an object produces `"[object Object]"` bytes and downstream encode/decode errors; filename hardcoded `response.json`.
- web: `file.writeFile('response.json', data)` with an object — no visible effect.
- Bonus: `ResponseBody.tsx` line ~112 contains a malformed comment (`\ TODO: ...`) to clean up.

**Design**:
- Define a proper payload type in core: `interface ResponseDownloadPayload { body: string; fileName: string; contentType: string }` and type `onDownloadResponse(payload: ResponseDownloadPayload)` through `ResponseBody` → `RequestEditor` (both platforms) → `App.tsx`.
- **vscode handler**: encode `payload.body` (text path) or decode base64 (binary path, aligned with how `ResponseBody` distinguishes text/binary) → `file.downloadResponse(bytes, payload.fileName, payload.contentType)` (save-dialog flow already exists).
- **web handler**: implement a real browser download (Blob + object URL + anchor click) in the web file adapter (new `downloadFile(bytes/text, fileName, contentType)` adapter method if `writeFile` can't express it), honoring fileName/contentType.
- Verify parity with Copy (which works) for JSON/text and add a binary-content test path. Component tests assert the payload contract.

---

### FEAT-009 — Request Cancellation

**Current state**: no abort path. `HttpService.execute` awaits axios with no signal; the UI spinner runs until timeout.

**Design** (end-to-end, both platforms):
- **HttpService** (`packages/shared`): maintain `Map<requestId, AbortController>`; pass `controller.signal` to both axios call sites (`send` participates only if trivially threadable — primary target is `execute`). New `cancel(requestId): boolean`. On abort, return a well-formed `HttpResponseResult` with `status: 0, statusText: 'Cancelled'` (UI-friendly, not an unhandled error); always clean the registry in `finally`. Requests already carry a unique `id` (the tab id) — use it as the cancellation key.
- **vscode**: new `cancelHttpRequest` message (`{ requestId }`) in MessageHandler → `httpService.cancel(id)`. `vsCodeAdapter.http` gains `cancelRequest(requestId)`.
- **web/server**: new route `POST /api/http/:id/cancel` → `httpService.cancel(id)`; `webAdapter.http.cancelRequest` calls it. (Server-side axios is what must abort — client-side fetch abort alone wouldn't stop the outbound request.)
- **core adapter contract**: `IHttpAdapter` gains `cancelRequest(requestId: string): Promise<Result<void, string>>`; mock adapter updated.
- **UI**: while a tab `isProcessing`, the Send button becomes Cancel (icon swap) or shows an adjacent cancel control; on click → `http.cancelRequest(tabId)`; response area shows "Request cancelled". Cancelled requests still appear in history (already saved pre-send).
- Tests: HttpService cancel unit test (abort mid-flight via never-resolving mock), MessageHandler routing test, UI state test.

---

### FEAT-010 — Save Keyboard Shortcut

**Design**:
- `Ctrl+S` / `Cmd+S` (`e.key === 's' && (e.ctrlKey || e.metaKey)`) in the request editor triggers the existing save flow for the **active tab**: if the request has a `sourceRef`, save directly (same as the save button); otherwise open the Save wizard. `preventDefault()` to suppress the browser/VS Code default.
- Implement as a small core hook (`useSaveShortcut(onSave)`) attached at the request-editor container level (not document-global, to avoid hijacking save in Environments/Flows panes — they can adopt the hook later, tracked in TODO).
- vscode webview: keydown reaches the webview when focused; verify no conflict with VS Code's own Ctrl+S (webview-focused keystrokes stay in the webview; no `package.json` keybinding needed).
- Note in docs a shortcuts section (single entry for now, structured for growth).

---

### FEAT-011 — Request Editor Spacing Polish + Copy/Paste Relocation

Per the annotated screenshot (`.github/images/waveclient_req_page_margin_issue.png`, markers 1–4):
- **(1) Editor chrome margins**: tighten outer left/top padding around the method/URL row and breadcrumb header in both `RequestEditor.tsx` files and the shared core sections — reduce to minimal Tailwind steps (e.g., `p-4` → `p-2`/`px-3`) while staying visually balanced. Recent commit `cd089e2` started this; finish the pass for top/bottom and the response divider (4).
- **(2)+(3) Params/Headers copy-paste toolbar**: remove the standalone toolbar `div` above the tables in `RequestParams.tsx` and `RequestHeaders.tsx`; render the Copy/Paste icon buttons **inside the table header**, in the Actions column header cell (right-aligned next to the "Actions" label), preserving tooltips, disabled states, and the existing clipboard-adapter logic. This reclaims a full row of vertical space.
- **(4) Response section**: same margin tightening for the response status bar and tab content padding.
- Pure presentational change — no behavioral changes; update affected component tests/snapshots. Keep margins consistent across Params/Headers/Body/Validation tabs.

---

### FEAT-012 — Versioning Strategy & Documentation Sync

**Current state**: every package is `0.0.1`; versioning semantics are undefined.

**Design** (documented in a new `docs/versioning.md`, summarized in README):
- **Four independent version tracks**:
  1. **VS Code app** — `packages/vscode` (`wave-client-vscode`); the marketplace-facing version.
  2. **Web app** — `packages/web`.
  3. **Core platform** — `packages/core`, `packages/shared`, `packages/arena` (and other commonly-consumed libs) versioned **in lockstep** as one platform version.
  4. **Wave schemas** — collection schema and environment schema versions (FEAT-001), independent of all package versions; bumped only on persisted-shape changes, with migration notes.
- Semver semantics for each track; apps may bump without platform bumps and vice versa. `server`/`mcp-server` follow the core platform track unless/until they ship independently.
- Manual process for now (no changesets/CI automation — tracked in TODO): a "how to bump" checklist in `docs/versioning.md`.
- **Docs sync for this plan**: update `README.md`, `docs/` (import formats now list `.http` semantics + Postman environments, request cancellation, save shortcut, schema docs reference) as each feature lands — per memory, docs are part of each feature, and this feature is the final consistency sweep.

## 6. Security Considerations

No plan-specific concerns beyond memory. Notable touchpoints: imported files (collections/environments/.http) are untrusted input — FEAT-001/002/006 add Zod/structured validation at exactly those boundaries, which strengthens the existing posture. Postman environment imports may contain secrets in plain values; imported variables marked `secret` follow the existing `EnvironmentVariable.type = 'secret'` handling.

## 7. Architecture & Design (Plan-Specific)

- **Request/response over fire-and-forget for mutations**: `vsCodeAdapter.saveCollection` is converted from optimistic fire-and-forget postMessage to the `sendAndWait` pattern used by every other mutation — the silent-drop bug (FEAT-003) is the canonical argument for this rule.
- **ID-keyed persistence and store operations**: `filename` is the storage key, `waveId`/`item.id` the logical identities; display `name` is never used as a lookup key in slices or services after this plan.
- **Transformer pattern extended to environments** (FEAT-006): same registry/auto-detect shape as collection transformers, kept in `packages/core` so both platforms share it.
- **Pull-based refresh stays the pattern** (FEAT-007): mutate via adapter → reload via adapter → `set*` slice. No new event/subscription mechanism.
- **Cancellation via id-keyed AbortController registry** in `HttpService` (FEAT-009) — works identically for the vscode message channel and the server REST channel.

## 8. Coding Standards (Plan-Specific Overrides)

No overrides — see memory.

## 9. Testing Strategy (Plan-Specific)

- **Regression-first**: every verified root cause above gets a failing-then-passing test (missing `saveCollection` handler, object-vs-string download payload, stale history, id regeneration on move).
- `.http` parser: table-driven unit tests with fixture files covering the full Microsoft syntax matrix (FEAT-002 list).
- Transformer/schema validation: fixture-based tests for Postman env, Wave env, malformed inputs.
- UI: RTL tests for the move/save wizard (current path, cross-collection folder listing), cancel button state machine, copy/paste buttons in table header.
- Cross-platform: vscode `MessageHandler` tests + web adapter tests for every new/changed message and route.

## 10. Logging Strategy (Plan-Specific)

- Log (with context: filename, waveId/item id, operation) every collection mutation failure path in `CollectionService`/MessageHandler — these were silent before.
- Log cancellation events in `HttpService` (request id, elapsed time at abort). Never log request bodies/headers (may contain secrets).
